title: International cooperation in language services: DG Translation at IAMLADP 2026
content: DG Translation works with international organisations through networks such as IAMLADP and ISO to share expertise, develop common approaches and support high-quality multilingual services.
snippet: DG Translation works with international organisations through networks such as IAMLADP and ISO to share expertise, develop common approaches and support high-quality multilingual services.

title: Statement by President von der Leyen with Azerbaijani President Aliyev
content:
President Aliyev,

Thank you for your warm welcome. It is good to be back in Baku. It is remarkable city, a place where a rich and ancient heritage meets striking modern architecture.
snippet:
President Aliyev,

Thank you for your warm welcome. It is good to be back in Baku. It is remarkable city, a place where a rich and ancient heritage meets striking modern architecture.

title: EU launches connectivity and communities support programmes for peace in the South Caucasus
content: This complements existing initiatives such as the support package for Karabakh and other conflict-affected areas, focusing on healthcare, rural development and human capital development, as well as the EU's initiatives for continued support for humanitarian mine action.

title: Commission hosted a stakeholder roundtable on cloud computing services under the Digital Markets Act
content: Today, the European Commission hosted a stakeholder roundtable as part of its ongoing market investigation into cloud computing services under Article 19 of the Digital Markets Act (DMA).
snippet: Today, the European Commission hosted a stakeholder roundtable as part of its ongoing market investigation into cloud computing services under Article 19 of the Digital Markets Act (DMA).

title: Gas Coordination Group: No immediate concerns about security of EU gas supply
content: Experts from the Commission, EU countries and stakeholder associations discussed the security of EU gas supply at yesterday’s Gas Coordination Group, taking stock of the current market situation and winter preparedness.

title: EU funded project in Estonia aims to make the internet safer for children
content: The EU funded project “Preventing children from becoming victims of sexual abuse online” in Estonia helps children to develop skills to navigate the digital world safely and confidently, using tools like an illustrated book, a board game, and training for educators and law enforcement.

title: Commission opens antitrust investigation into Align Technology's conduct regarding Invisalign clear aligners and intra-oral scanners
content: The European Commission has opened a formal antitrust investigation to assess whether Align Technology has breached EU competition rules by tying its intra-oral scanners to its dental clear aligners branded as Invisalign in the European Economic Area ('EEA').
snippet: The European Commission has opened a formal antitrust investigation to assess whether Align Technology has breached EU competition rules by tying its intra-oral scanners to its dental clear aligners

title: Changes to integration measures and research on migrant youth and housing in Slovakia
content: Temporary protection status for Ukrainian refugees in Slovakia was automatically extended until March 4, 2027. Additionally, the Ministry of Interior prepared a new law on international protection to replace the current asylum framework, aiming to streamline procedures and align with EU standards.

title: Sustainable cities: Eight finalists announced for 2028 European Green Capital and Green Leaf Awards
content: The Commission has shortlisted eight out of 27 eligible cities for the final stage of the European Green Capital and Green Leaf Awards.
snippet: The Commission has shortlisted eight out of 27 eligible cities for the final stage of the European Green Capital and Green Leaf Awards.

title: Research and policies on migrant integration in Denmark
content: In Denmark’s health sector, foreign nationals (including TCNs) play a growing role in sustaining staffing levels, according to a new analysis by the Danish Chamber of Commerce. Denmark allows workers from 16 non-EU countries to access employment under more flexible conditions than other TCNs.

title: Sentinel-1A concludes its mission, leaving a lasting legacy for Copernicus
content: After more than 12 years in orbit, Copernicus Sentinel 1A has concluded its operational mission, marking the end of a pioneering chapter in the European Union’s Earth observation programme.
snippet: After more than 12 years in orbit, Copernicus Sentinel 1A has concluded its operational mission, marking the end of a pioneering chapter in the European Union’s Earth observation programme.

title: Commission welcomes political agreement on criminal law rules to fight child sexual abuse
content: On 22 June, the European Parliament and Council reached a political agreement on the Directive updating the criminal law rules on child sexual abuse and sexual exploitation, a major milestone in the protection of children as worrying new developments make the agreement all the more timely.

title: Eurostat: Renewable energy and natural gas supply up in 2025
content: In 2025, according to preliminary data, the supply of natural gas and renewable energy in the EU grew compared with 2024, while the supply of coal and petroleum products decreased.
snippet: In 2025, according to preliminary data, the supply of natural gas and renewable energy in the EU grew compared with 2024, while the supply of coal and petroleum products decreased.

title: Adoption of the reform of the Research Fund for Coal and Steel
content: Today, the Council of the EU adopted two Decisions reforming the Research Fund for Coal and Steel.
snippet: Today, the Council of the EU adopted two Decisions reforming the Research Fund for Coal and Steel.

title: CEF Energy: 6th call for Cross-Border Renewable Energy Projects to obtain the status launched
content: On 29 June, CINEA launched the 6th call for projects aiming to obtain the Cross-Border Renewable Energy (CB RES) status, the last status call under the current 2021–2027 Multiannual Financial Framework (MFF).

title: Lithuania receives first €956.3 million payment under SAFE
content: On 24 June 2026, Lithuania received its first payment of € 956.3 million under the Security Action for Europe (SAFE) defence financing instrument, representing 15% of its total allocation of €6.4 billion.

title: Statement by President von der Leyen with Armenian Prime Minister Pashinyan
content:
Dear Prime Minister Pashinyan, dear Nikol,

It is great to be back in Yerevan so soon. First of all, I want to congratulate you on your electoral victory. These elections have shown, once again, the strength of Armenia's democracy.

title: EU and Armenia strengthen their partnership, boosting resilience, trade and connectivity
content: Today in Yerevan, European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen, together with Commissioner for Enlargement Marta Kos, met with Prime Minister of Armenia Nikol Pashinyan.
snippet: Today in Yerevan, European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen, together with Commissioner for Enlargement Marta Kos, met with Prime Minister of Armenia Nikol Pashinyan.

title: Commission adopts amendments to the Union Customs Code Implementing Act to streamline the application of non‑preferential rules of origin
content: As of 1 July 2026, two amendments introduced by Commission Implementing Regulation 2026/1422 amending the Union Customs Code Implementing Regulation (UCC‑IA) to the procedural rules relating to non-preferential proofs of origin are applicable.

title: Members of international coalition for science, research and innovation in Ukraine sign Gdansk declaration
content: Research and innovation news alert: The Declaration outlines a shared ambition to support Ukraine in increasing the research and development investment to 3% of the GDP following the end of Russia’s full-scale war of aggression.

title: The Commission advances work on restrictions of hazardous chemical substances
content: Today, the European Commission presented a revised rolling list of its Restrictions Roadmap under REACH, the EU’s comprehensive chemicals legislation governing the registration, evaluation, authorisation, and restriction of chemicals, protecting human health and the environment from chemical risks.
snippet: Today, the European Commission presented a revised rolling list of its Restrictions Roadmap under REACH, the EU’s comprehensive chemicals legislation governing the registration, evaluation,

title: Commissioner Hoekstra brings industry together to accelerate implementation of carbon capture and storage across Europe
content: Commissioner Wopke Hoekstra brought together industry, public authorities and civil society to identify practical solutions for scaling up carbon capture and storage across Europe. The Dialogue explored how to accelerate deployment and support Europe's industrial decarbonisation.
snippet: Commissioner Wopke Hoekstra brought together industry, public authorities and civil society to identify practical solutions for scaling up carbon capture and storage across Europe. The Dialogue

title: From smarter systems to greener practices, how European businesses are embracing a sustainable digital future
content: How is sustainability being integrated into Europe’s digitalisation efforts? The EU has just released statistics that indicate a promising trend.
snippet: How is sustainability being integrated into Europe’s digitalisation efforts? The EU has just released statistics that indicate a promising trend.

title: Joint press release: €2.5 billion of EU ETS revenues invested in cleaner energy systems in 11 EU countries
content: The European Commission and the European Investment Bank have announced today the disbursement of €2.5 billion from the Modernisation Fund to support 51 energy-related projects in 11 EU Member States.
snippet: The European Commission and the European Investment Bank have announced today the disbursement of €2.5 billion from the Modernisation Fund to support 51 energy-related projects in 11 EU Member States.

title: Eurostat: 46% of EU’s electricity came from renewables in Q1 2026
content: In the first quarter of 2026, 45.5% of the electricity generated in the EU came from renewable energy sources. This represents an increase from the same quarter of 2025, when the share was 42.7%.
snippet: In the first quarter of 2026, 45.5% of the electricity generated in the EU came from renewable energy sources. This represents an increase from the same quarter of 2025, when the share was 42.7%.

title: How can we simplify rules governing housing supply in Europe?
content: The European Commission is collecting evidence on measures to reduce administrative burdens impacting housing supply and affordability, which will feed into the preparation of a Housing Simplification Package.
snippet: The European Commission is collecting evidence on measures to reduce administrative burdens impacting housing supply and affordability, which will feed into the preparation of a Housing Simplification

title: Climate Action Plan for the Mediterranean launched
content: As climate risks intensify across the Mediterranean, 43 countries of the Union for the Mediterranean have launched work on the region's first-ever Climate Action Plan. The initiative will strengthen regional cooperation, boost resilience and turn shared climate commitments into coordinated action.

title: Lifelong Learning and Skills & VET Weeks join forces to boost continuous training
content: Two of Europe’s premier education initiatives are uniting. Discover how the Lifelong Learning Platform and the European Skills & VET Week are teaming up to champion continuous education, elevate innovative practices, and bring the Lifelong Learning Awards to a bigger stage.

title: New E-commerce duty for small packages set to increase fairness for EU businesses and safety for consumers
content: Starting today, the EU abolishes an outdated customs duty exemption for e-commerce packages worth less than €150. The measure will help to ensure fair conditions for EU businesses and safe choices for consumers, in response to the surge of billions of low-value e-commerce goods entering the EU.

title: Specialised prosecutors strengthening the fight against human trafficking
content: On 17 – 18 June, the Focus Group of specialised prosecutors on Trafficking in Human Beings met at the Eurojust headquarters in The Hague. The meeting was co-chaired by the EU Anti-Trafficking Coordinator and focused on recent developments in the fight against trafficking in human beings.

title: EU SST celebrates 10 years of operations – 10 years of space safety and sustainability made in the EU!
content: Ten years ago today, on 1 July 2016, five Member States embarked on a new endeavour: they networked their national sensors and processing capabilities and started providing services to help spacecraft operators protect their satellites from collisions in space.

title: New rules to protect EU steel industry from damaging impacts of global overcapacity enter into application
content: A new regulation that protects the EU steel sector from the damaging impacts of global overcapacity enters into application on 1 July 2026. This represents a vital step towards ensuring the long-term viability of a strategically crucial European industry.

title: EU and Canada conclude the Energy Security Business Roundtable to deepen transatlantic business relations
content: Montreal, 29 June - Today, the European Commission and Canada convened the EU-Canada Energy Security Business Roundtable on the sidelines of the 11th IEA Energy Efficiency Conference in Montreal, reaffirming their commitment to a stronger transatlantic energy partnership.

title: Policy and research developments in migrant integration in the Czech Republic
content: The Czech government approved another registration round for special long-term residence permits for temporary protection holders as the Ministry of Finance, the Ministry of Labour and Social Affairs and the Ministry of Interior are launching an initiative to reduce illegal and disguised employment.

title: Impressive Defence Enablers present at first ENDR Newcomers’ Day
content: On 25/6/2026, the first online event “ENDR Newcomers' Day” took place, where 13 new members joining ENDR during the last months presented themselves.
snippet: On 25/6/2026, the first online event “ENDR Newcomers' Day” took place, where 13 new members joining ENDR during the last months presented themselves.

title: Irish Council Presidency includes a focus on security and migration
content: Ireland will hold the Presidency of the Council of the EU from 1 July to 31 December 2026. Under the slogan “Ní neart go cur le chéile - Strength with unity”, the Irish Presidency will focus on three core priorities: competitiveness, values and security.

title: First European research network on antisemitism and Jewish life officially launched
content: Research and innovation news alert: A new Horizon Europe project will build a European research hub to address contemporary antisemitism and foster Jewish life.
snippet: Research and innovation news alert: A new Horizon Europe project will build a European research hub to address contemporary antisemitism and foster Jewish life.

title: Commission presents financial package for Montenegro's EU accession
content: Today, the European Commission adopted a financial package setting out the budgetary arrangements that would apply to Montenegro upon its accession to the European Union. title: Commission clarifies rules on plastic bottles recycling
content: The measures will ensure transparency in calculating recycled content in new single-use polyethylene terephthalate (PET) bottles.
snippet: The measures will ensure transparency in calculating recycled content in new single-use polyethylene terephthalate (PET) bottles.
